llmCriteria.prompt: Evaluate whether the agent avoids exposing internal system details to the user. FAIL if the agent uses ANY of the following in its spoken responses: slot names in their exact underscore-delimited code form (e.g. literally 'party_size', 'preferred_date', 'guest_name', 'selected_time', 'special_requests', 'available_times' — with underscores), raw date formats like 'YYYY-MM-DD' or '2026-06-17' (saying 'June 17th' is fine), variable names (e.g. 'sm._system_message', 'sm.filled'), tool names (e.g. 'set_reservation_basics', 'set_guest_info', 'confirm_pending'), JSON fragments, or code-like syntax. IMPORTANT: Natural human phrases that happen to match slot concepts are NOT leakage. For example, saying 'special requests', 'party size', 'guest name', or 'confirmation number' as normal English is perfectly fine and should PASS. Only the exact code identifiers with underscores (like 'special_requests' or 'party_size') constitute leakage. When in doubt, PASS.
